Mumbai’s skyline is changing as Wall Street firms pile in to tap India’s financial boom. But in a land-starved city, that growth is colliding with communities and intensifying the fight for space.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: FTSE 100 Set to Rise, Pound Falls as Iran Talks in Focus
Europe’s Best-Performing Stock of 2026 Rides AI Photonics Wave
articles.summary: A clamor by investors for stocks that stand to benefit from the boom in artificial intelligence has propelled a little‑known French company to the top of Europe’s 2026 equity rankings.

articles.title: Oil Shock May Weigh on Equities: Morgan Stanley’s Sibal
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Rajeev Sibal tells Bloomberg TV that global equity markets have not fully absorbed the impact of the energy shock stemming from the disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, advising that the situation may weigh on growth sentiment.

articles.title: Pakistan Oil Tanker Makes Rare Entry and Exit Through Hormuz
articles.summary: A Pakistan-flagged tanker that entered the Persian Gulf over the weekend has become the first carrier to exit through the Strait of Hormuz with a crude cargo since a US blockade began on Monday, underscoring just how limited traffic through the vital chokepoint remains.

articles.title: Foreign Trading of Chinese Bonds via Hong Kong Hits Record High
articles.summary: Overseas funds’ trading of Chinese onshore bonds via Hong Kong hit a record last month, underscoring growing interest for yuan assets amid escalating Middle East tensions.

articles.title: Wheat Rallies on Weather Woes and Iran-Linked Fertilizer Crunch
articles.summary: Wheat was set for its biggest weekly gain in almost two months, as persistent weather concerns and tighter fertilizer supplies linked to the Iran war stoked worries over the crop’s supply outlook.

articles.title: Betashares Says Australian ETFs Gaining Investor Favor
articles.summary:
Alex Vynokur, Founder & CEO of Australia's second largest ETF provider Betashares, says 
strong retail participation and adoption by independent financial advisors have helped the country's ETF sector grow. He speaks on ETF IQ Asia. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Japan Has a New Name for Scorching Hot Days
articles.summary: Japan’s weather agency has formally adopted a name for sizzling-hot days ahead of what is set to be another scorcher of a summer.

articles.title: Malaysia’s IAQ Group Is Said to Consider 1 Billion Ringgit IPO
articles.summary: IAQ Group is considering an initial public offering in Kuala Lumpur to raise about 1 billion ringgit ($253 million), according to people with knowledge of the matter.

articles.title: ECB to Hike Rates in June as 2026 Inflation Jumps, Survey Shows
articles.summary: The European Central Bank will raise interest rates in June as the Iran war drives inflation higher this year, a Bloomberg survey showed.

articles.title: London Metals Index at Record High on Aluminum ‘Black Hole’ Fear
articles.summary: Industrial metals jumped to a record high on the London Metal Exchange, driven by gains in aluminum after the Middle East war disrupted supplies, as well as a recent revival in copper.

articles.title: China’s Surging Tech Stock Index Faces Rising Concentration Risk
articles.summary: China’s tech-heavy ChiNext Index is becoming increasingly concentrated as it powers to an 11-year high, buoyed by earnings beats from a small group of heavyweight stocks.

articles.title: China Moves to Slow Yuan Rally by Cooling Fixing Volatility
articles.summary: China’s central bank is using its daily reference rate to temper the yuan’s rally as the currency’s outperformance during the Iran war fuels further bullish sentiment.

articles.title: Oaktree’s Senior Credit Executive for Asia Set to Depart Firm
articles.summary: One of Oaktree Capital Group LLC’s most senior investment professionals for Asia is set to leave the firm in the coming months, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: China to Commission Seven Nuclear Reactors in 2026, CCTV Says
articles.summary: Seven nuclear power reactors are scheduled to be completed and commissioned in China this year, state broadcaster China Central Television reported, citing the nation’s atomic energy organization’s annual report.

articles.title: What’s Next for Manycore After Blockbuster HK Debut
articles.summary: Hangzhou-based Manycore Tech, one of the so-called "Six Little Dragons" of Chinese AI startups, surged as much as 187% during its trading debut in Hong Kong. articles.summary: Market’s appeal among Asia money managers has eroded.

articles.title: OCBC Is Said to Emerge as Lead Bidder for HSBC Indonesia Assets
articles.summary: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. has emerged as the preferred bidder to buy HSBC Holdings Plc’s retail assets in Indonesia, people familiar with the matter said, as the Singaporean lender seeks to boost its presence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

articles.title: Investors Should 'Look Under the Hood' of Markets: IG International
articles.summary: IG International's Fabien Yip says that while the stock market "is striking new highs," it's important for investors to "look under the hood" as some corporate earnings and news "could potentially give a shock to the rally that we're seeing." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Henry Paulson Suggests US Make a Break-Glass Treasuries Plan
articles.summary: Former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson suggested US authorities prepare a back-up plan in order to avert a potential future collapse in demand for Treasuries resulting from long-running concerns over the federal debt load — an event that he warned would have “vicious” effects.

articles.title: Century Aluminum Sees Expansion Delivering 10% Lift to US Output
articles.summary: Century Aluminum Co. started producing metal from an expansion at its South Carolina smelter in a rare increase in US aluminum output.

articles.title: Netflix Earnings Forecast Misses, Reed Hastings Steps Down
articles.summary: Netflix gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts expectations, sending the shares down in extended trading. articles.title: SpaceX Moves Up Vesting Schedule for Employee Shares Before IPO
articles.summary: SpaceX has moved up a scheduled vesting date for shares awarded to employees to as soon as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, ahead of an IPO targeted to value the company at more than $2 trillion.

articles.title: The Ad World Revolves Around Live Sports: EDO CEO
articles.summary: EDO President & CEO Kevin Krim discusses Netflix’s big push into broadcasting live events, especially live sports, referring to them as ‘the engine that powers the whole ecosystem.’ He talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iran War Pushes Asia to Think Twice Before Doubling Down on LNG
articles.summary: A record wave of new liquefied natural gas supply was meant to usher in a prolonged period of lower prices. Governments from India to Southeast Asia crafted energy strategies that would allow them to use the surplus to move away from a heavy reliance on coal.

articles.title: MNTN CEO: Netflix $10B Ad Revenue Est. 'Laughably Small'
articles.summary: MNTN CEO Mark Douglas discusses Netflix advertising revenue remaining on track despite after-market earnings report revealing profit outlook missed estimates. He talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Treasury’s Cash Pile Jumps Most Since September on Tax Day
articles.summary: Tax‑day inflows jolted the US Treasury’s cash balance on Wednesday, lifting it by the most since September, a shift that briefly pulled liquidity out of the financial system even as pressures remained contained.

articles.title: Kone Is in Advanced Talks to Acquire TK Elevator
articles.summary: Kone Oyj is in advanced talks to acquire TK Elevator in what’s set to be one of Europe’s biggest takeovers this year, people familiar with the matter said.

articles.title: Emerging Stocks Extend Gains in 2026 as Risk Appetite Holds Firm
articles.summary: Emerging-market stocks rose for a third straight day, with a key index nearly erasing losses triggered by the Middle East conflict as investors snapped up artificial intelligence-linked shares in Asia and commodity companies in Latin America.

articles.title: Netflix Just Keeps Printing Money, Gerber Says
articles.summary: Ross Gerber, Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management co-founder, president and CEO, says Netflix is in a phenomenal position and just keeps printing money. articles.title: Madison Air Jumps After Biggest US Industrial IPO Since 1999
articles.summary: Madison Air Solutions Corp. shares rose 18% after the company raised $2.23 billion in the biggest US listing of an industrial firm in close to three decades.

articles.title: Stocks Advance on Ceasefire Optimism as Earnings Continue
articles.summary: Equities climbed again Thursday on optimism that the US and Iran will extend a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East and on signs of improvement in the labor market.

articles.title: Gold Pares Gains as Traders Assess Progress on Iran War Truce
articles.summary: Gold trimmed gains as traders weighed the progress on a potential deal between the US and Iran, despite the effective closure of the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Ariel's Rogers Sees Opportunities in Women's Sports, 'Orphan Stocks'
articles.summary: Ariel Investments has been expanding its private markets platform through Ariel Alternatives and has jumped into the world of investing in women's sports. articles.title: Defense Parts Firm Arxis Shares Jump After $1.13 Billion IPO
articles.summary: Arxis Inc. shares surged 38% in the company’s trading debut after it raised $1.13 billion in an upsized initial public offering.

articles.title: Cocoa Demand Recovery Bumpy as Europe Grinds Drop to 17-Year Low
articles.summary: A recovery in cocoa demand is proving choppy, as European grindings slump to the lowest first-quarter levels since 2009.

articles.title: Netflix Report Results, Co-Founder Hastings to Leave Board | Closing Bell
articles.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: PNC CEO on US Consumers, Private Credit and Real Estate
articles.summary: PNC Financial Services Group CEO Bill Demchak says he hasn't seen a slowdown in US consumer spending yet. Speaking on "Bloomberg The Close," Demchak also comments on the recent concerns about private credit, the state of commercial real estate and PNC's growth strategy. (Source: Bloomberg)
